description: The CodeBoarding system orchestrates a comprehensive code analysis and documentation generation workflow. It begins with the Application Orchestrator & Repository Manager initializing projects, managing code repositories, and detecting changes. This orchestrator leverages the Incremental Analysis Engine to optimize performance by caching and re-analyzing only modified code. The core intelligence resides in the LLM Agent Core, which uses large language models to interpret code and analysis results. The agent interacts with the codebase and static analysis data through the Agent Tooling Interface, which in turn queries the Static Code Analyzer for deep structural and behavioral insights. Finally, the LLM Agent's findings are passed to the Documentation & Diagram Generator to produce user-friendly documentation and visual architectural diagrams.

components.name: Application Orchestrator & Repository Manager
components.description: Manages the overall application lifecycle, including project initialization, repository operations (cloning, updating), change detection, and orchestrating the analysis workflow. It also handles the initial setup and environment configuration for the analysis tools.

components.name: Application Orchestrator & Repository Manager
components.description: Manages the overall application lifecycle, including project initialization, repository operations (cloning, updating), change detection, and orchestrating the analysis workflow. It acts as the primary control surface for CLI and remote processing.

components.name: Static Analysis & Environment Manager
components.description: Ensures the execution environment is ready by downloading/installing Language Server Protocol (LSP) servers and verifying platform‑specific binaries. It executes deterministic static analysis (LSP/CFG) to extract structural code data.

components.name: Analysis Synthesizer
components.description: The "brain" of the data layer. It parses raw outputs from various static analysis tools and transforms them into the UnifiedAnalysisJson schema, mapping internal IDs to human‑readable component names.

components.name: Metadata & Persistence Store
components.description: Provides a persistence layer using DuckDB to track job history, status updates, and analysis metadata. This ensures reproducibility and allows the system to skip analysis for unchanged files.

components.name: Telemetry & Monitoring Service
components.description: A cross‑cutting service that wraps execution steps in a MonitorContext. It specifically tracks LLM token usage and streams real‑time execution statistics to the filesystem for external monitoring.

components_relations.relation: triggers environment verification and initiates static analysis
records job start/end times and updates status
passes raw structural data and LSP outputs for normalization
provides MonitorContext that wraps execution steps to capture performance and token metrics

components.name: LLM Agent Core
components.description: The intelligent core responsible for driving the code analysis and documentation generation using large language models. It orchestrates agent workflows, manages interactions with various tools, and structures the analysis insights.

components.name: Agent Orchestrator
components.description: The central coordinator (CodeBoardingAgent) that manages the analysis lifecycle, initializes LLM providers, and dispatches tasks to specialized agents.

components.name: Specialized Analysis Agents
components.description: A suite of domain‑specific agents (AbstractionAgent, DetailsAgent, MetaAgent) that analyze code for architectural patterns, low‑level logic, and project‑wide context.

components.name: Multi-Provider Prompt Factory
components.description: Generates provider‑specific instructions (Claude, Gemini, GPT) to ensure consistent agent behavior across different LLM backends.

components.name: Validation Engine
components.description: A quality‑gate component that verifies LLM outputs against static analysis data to ensure every code cluster is correctly mapped and relationships are consistent.

components.name: Analysis Planner
components.description: Strategic component that determines the depth of analysis, identifying which components require further "drill‑down" or expansion based on initial findings.

components.name: LLM Gateway
components.description: Manages API configurations, model selection, and the initialization of LangChain‑compatible clients for various providers.

components.name: Structured Response Models
components.description: Pydantic‑based schemas (e.g., AnalysisInsights) that define the strict data contract for all LLM communications.

components.name: Cluster-to-Component Mapper
components.description: Provides the logic (ClusterMethodsMixin) to translate raw CFG clusters and file paths into logical architectural components.

components_relations.relation: requests tailored system and user prompts
dispatches specific code segments and metadata for analysis
utilizes mapping utilities to resolve raw data into internal component representation
submits LLM‑generated insights for verification
signals validation failures, triggering corrective re‑prompts
provides current analysis state to determine if additional drill‑down passes are required
references Pydantic schemas to embed format instructions within prompts
requests authenticated and configured LLM instances for executing agent tasks

components.name: Static Code Analyzer
components.description: Performs deep structural and behavioral analysis of the codebase across multiple programming languages. It extracts information like call graphs, code structure, and identifies code quality issues, including unused code.

components.name: Project Environment Scanner
components.description: Detects project structures (Maven, Gradle, TSConfig) and identifies programming languages to configure the analysis environment.

components.name: LSP Client Provider
components.description: Orchestrates communication with language‑specific servers to extract semantic symbols, diagnostics, and file‑level metadata.

components.name: Call Graph Builder
components.description: Constructs a global structural topology by linking symbols across files, identifying behavioral dependencies.

components.name: Graph Clustering Engine
components.description: Applies adaptive clustering algorithms to the call graph to group code into logical, high‑level functional modules.

components.name: Code Quality Analyzer
components.description: Performs deep behavioral analysis to identify dead (unused) code and structural smells such as God Classes or high coupling.

components.name: Analysis Result Manager
components.description: Serializes findings into a unified model and manages the AnalysisCache to enable incremental re‑analysis.

components_relations.relation: identifies technology stack, triggering initialization
feeds semantic symbols and references
provides raw graph topology
provides clustered boundaries
provides reachability data
outputs health reports and diagnostics
sends direct file‑level diagnostics and symbol metadata

components.name: Agent Tooling Interface
components.description: Provides a set of specialized tools that allow the LLM Agent Core to interact with the codebase, query static analysis results, and perform specific actions within the project context.

components.name: Toolkit Orchestrator
components.description: The central registry and factory that instantiates tools and exposes them to agents via a unified interface.

components.name: Repository Context
components.description: A shared-state container providing the repository root, ignore patterns, and handles to the static analysis engine.

components.name: Structural Navigator
components.description: Generates hierarchical tree views of the directory layout, respecting .gitignore to provide a high-level project map.

components.name: Source Content Provider
components.description: Supplies raw file content and symbol-aware source extraction for specific classes or functions.

components.name: Flow & Logic Analyzer
components.description: Traces execution paths, generates Control Flow Graphs (CFGs), and maps method-level call relationships.

components.name: Architectural Mapper
components.description: Extracts high-level structural metadata, including class inheritance hierarchies and package-level dependencies.

components.name: Change Detector
components.description: Performs delta-analysis by reading Git diffs, allowing agents to focus on incremental code changes.

components.name: Documentation Reader
components.description: Specifically targets and extracts content from project documentation (READMEs, /docs) for contextual grounding.

queries

components.name: Incremental Analysis Engine
components.description: Optimizes analysis performance by managing the caching of static analysis results and orchestrating re-analysis only for changed parts of the codebase, ensuring efficiency and speed.

components.name: IncrementalUpdater
components.description: The central orchestrator that manages the lifecycle of an incremental update. It coordinates between state validation, impact analysis, and task generation.

components.name: AnalysisCache
components.description: Manages the persistence and retrieval of previous analysis results, including file hashes, metadata, and the existing architectural graph stored in DuckDB.

components.name: StateValidator
components.description: Performs granular comparison of current file system states against the AnalysisCache to identify added, modified, or deleted files.

components.name: ClusterChangeAnalyzer
components.description: Evaluates logical groupings (clusters) of code to determine if a change in one module necessitates the re-analysis of its parent or sibling clusters.

components.name: DependencyImpactMapper
components.description: Maps code-level changes to the high-level architectural graph to identify "blast zones"—unchanged areas that require documentation updates due to dependency shifts.

components.name: DeltaTaskGenerator
components.description: Translates the identified impact areas into a prioritized execution plan for the Static Analysis Engine and AI Agents.

components_relations.relation: invokes to determine which files have physically changed since the last run
retrieves historical hashes and metadata to perform delta detection
passes the detected file deltas to assess high-level architectural impact
traces how low-level code changes propagate through the dependency graph
queries the existing relationship graph to reconstruct the "blast zone" of a change
provides the final list of affected entities to create a minimized analysis queue for the AI agents

components.name: Documentation & Diagram Generator
components.description: Transforms the processed analysis data and insights into user-friendly documentation formats (e.g., Markdown, HTML) and generates visual representations like architectural diagrams.

components.name: Output Orchestration Facade
components.description: The central coordinator that selects and invokes specific renderers based on user configuration (Markdown, HTML, etc.) and queries the Documentation State Tracker for stale components.

components.name: Markdown/MDX Renderer
components.description: Transforms analysis insights into GitHub‑flavored Markdown and MDX for documentation portals, embedding diagram strings from the Mermaid Diagram Generator.

components.name: Sphinx/RST Renderer
components.description: Generates reStructuredText files and integrates with the Sphinx documentation framework, pulling styling assets from the Visual Asset Provider.

components.name: Mermaid Diagram Generator
components.description: Translates graph‑based metadata into Mermaid.js syntax for architectural and flow diagrams, updating the manifest with generated assets.

components.name: Interactive Visual Engine
components.description: Populates HTML templates with scripts and raw graph data for interactive visualizations (e.g., Cytoscape).

components.name: Visual Asset Provider
components.description: Manages CSS, styling assets, and static templates required for consistent visual output across renderers.

components.name: Documentation State Tracker
components.description: Interfaces with the Analysis Manifest to ensure incremental documentation updates and tracks file coverage, preventing redundant processing.

Orchestrator initiates analysis workflow, leveraging incremental updates based on detected code changes.
Orchestrator passes project context and triggers the main analysis workflow for the LLM Agent.
Incremental engine requests static analysis for specific code segments (new or changed).
Static analyzer provides analysis results to the incremental engine for caching.
LLM Agent invokes specialized tools to interact with the codebase and analysis data.
Agent tools query the static analysis engine for detailed code insights.
Static analysis engine provides requested data to the agent tools.
LLM Agent delivers structured analysis insights for documentation and diagram generation.
